headline: Microsoft bets big on AI in Australia with $18 billion investment
summary: Microsoft said on Thursday that it will invest A$25 billion ($17.9 billion) in Australia by the end of 2029 to boost computing and artificial intelligence capacity, ‌betting on growing demand for the technology in the country.  The U.S. tech giant's latest investment ‌reflects rising demand for AI technologies and positions Australia as a key growth market.  Microsoft said the investment will support the expansion ​of its Azure AI supercomputing and cloud infrastructure, strengthen cybersecurity and promote AI skills development across the country.

Microsoft said the investment will support the expansion ​of its Azure AI supercomputing and cloud infrastructure, strengthen cybersecurity and promote AI skills development across the country.

headline: Meta Faces New ESG Pressures As Board Shifts And Shares Screen Undervalued
summary: Shareholder groups have filed new proposals urging Meta Platforms to publish reports on human rights due diligence, online hate, climate impacts of AI data centers, H-1B worker practices, and executive pay linked to child safety. One proposal singles out Meta's role in alleged human rights issues in Gaza, calling for deeper disclosure on how its platforms are managed in conflict zones. Meta has also announced that two board members will not stand for re election, including Broadcom CEO Hock...

headline: EssilorLuxottica sales slide as investors turn wary of AI glasses

headline: Focus Turns to Social Media Stress as Tech Giants Face Scrutiny
summary: LOS ANGELES, April 23, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- A California jury in March 2026 found Meta and YouTube liable in a case alleging their platforms caused addiction and mental health harm in a young woman. The defendant, identified as Kaley, was awarded $6 million in what was viewed as a landmark verdict. headline: Applied Digital signs $7.5 billion AI data center lease with US hyperscaler
summary: Applied Digital has signed a long-term lease worth $7.5 billion with an unnamed U.S.-based hyperscaler at its new ‌Delta Forge 1 site, it said on Thursday, strengthening its ‌position as a data center provider for artificial intelligence workloads.  The 15-year lease covers 300 megawatts of computing capacity at the 430‑megawatt Delta Forge 1 site in southern U.S., the company said.  The deal makes the customer Applied Digital's second ‌U.S.-based investment‑grade hyperscale tenant ⁠across its data center sites and lifts total contracted lease revenue to more than $23 billion.

content.title: Applied Digital signs $7.5 billion AI data center lease with US hyperscaler
content.summary: Applied Digital has signed a long-term lease worth $7.5 billion with an unnamed U.S.-based hyperscaler at its new ‌Delta Forge 1 site, it said on Thursday, strengthening its ‌position as a data center provider for artificial intelligence workloads.  The 15-year lease covers 300 megawatts of computing capacity at the 430‑megawatt Delta Forge 1 site in southern U.S., the company said.  The deal makes the customer Applied Digital's second ‌U.S.-based investment‑grade hyperscale tenant ⁠across its data center sites and lifts total contracted lease revenue to more than $23 billion.

headline: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs in efficiency push amid AI spending surge
summary: Investing.com -- Meta Platforms Inc. plans to cut 10% of its workforce, or roughly 8,000 employees, in an effort to boost efficiency and offset heavy spending on artificial intelligence, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday.

content.title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs in efficiency push amid AI spending surge

headline: Meta Stock Slides on Report of Memo Announcing 10% Job Cuts
summary: Shares of tech giant  Meta  fell 3% Thursday after Bloomberg released what it said was a memo from the company’s chief people office, Janelle Gale, announcing cuts of thousands of workers.  The company is eliminating 10% of workers, which works out to about 8,000 people based on the company’s latest headcount of 78,865 as of December 31.

content.title: Meta Stock Slides on Report of Memo Announcing 10% Job Cuts
content.summary: Shares of tech giant  Meta  fell 3% Thursday after Bloomberg released what it said was a memo from the company’s chief people office, Janelle Gale, announcing cuts of thousands of workers.  The company is eliminating 10% of workers, which works out to about 8,000 people based on the company’s latest headcount of 78,865 as of December 31.

headline: Meta plans 10% layoffs as AI spending soars: source
summary: Meta plans to cut a tenth of its workforce, looking for productivity gains from its remaining workers as it invests heavily in artificial intelligence.Meta is locked in a bitter rivalry with other tech behemoths racing to invest heavily in AI, aiming to ensure the technology generates profits in the not-so-distant future.

headline: Meta slashes 8,000 jobs, or 10% of its workforce, as Microsoft offers buyouts
summary: Meta is laying off about 8,000 workers, or about 10% of its workforce, the company said Thursday as it continues to ramp up spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure and highly paid AI-expert hires.  Also Thursday, Microsoft said it was offering voluntary buyouts to thousands of its U.S. employees.  While an alternative to the sudden layoffs removing tech workers from peers like Meta and Oracle, the savings are likely tied to a similar industry upheaval that is requiring huge spending on the costs of artificial intelligence.

content.title: Meta slashes 8,000 jobs, or 10% of its workforce, as Microsoft offers buyouts
content.summary: Meta is laying off about 8,000 workers, or about 10% of its workforce, the company said Thursday as it continues to ramp up spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure and highly paid AI-expert hires.  Also Thursday, Microsoft said it was offering voluntary buyouts to thousands of its U.S. employees.  While an alternative to the sudden layoffs removing tech workers from peers like Meta and Oracle, the savings are likely tied to a similar industry upheaval that is requiring huge spending on the costs of artificial intelligence.

thumbnail.caption: El logo de Meta en una pantalla, en la conferencia LlamaCon 2025 en Menlo Park, California, el 29 de abril del 2025. (AP foto/Jeff Chiu)

headline: Why Meta is laying off 10% of its workforce
summary: The social media giant is planning to slash about 10% of its workforce even as its digital ad revenue continues to climb.

content.title: Why Meta is laying off 10% of its workforce
content.summary: The social media giant is planning to slash about 10% of its workforce even as its digital ad revenue continues to climb.

headline: Meta Stock Slides After Announcing 10% Job Cuts
summary: “We’re doing this as part of our continued effort to run the company more efficiently and to allow us to offset the other investments we’re making,”  Meta’s  chief people officer, Janelle Gale, wrote in a memo announcing the news to staff.  Laid off workers will receive severance of 16 weeks of base pay, plus two additional weeks for every year they have been with the company, Gale added.

content.title: Meta Stock Slides After Announcing 10% Job Cuts
content.summary: “We’re doing this as part of our continued effort to run the company more efficiently and to allow us to offset the other investments we’re making,”  Meta’s  chief people officer, Janelle Gale, wrote in a memo announcing the news to staff.  Laid off workers will receive severance of 16 weeks of base pay, plus two additional weeks for every year they have been with the company, Gale added.
